old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.48.1","title":"Implement 40-bit OSCORE sequences","description":"Rust OSCORE stores sender/replay sequence state as u32 even though RFC 8613 permits 40-bit Partial IVs. Five-byte PIVs above u32::MAX saturate/collapse, breaking replay ordering and interoperability. Convert sequence representation and vector consumers to bounded u64 (0..2^40-1), reject overlong/out-of-range values, and preserve terminal exhaustion without mutation.","acceptance_criteria":"Literal boundaries 0xff,0x100,u32::MAX,u32::MAX+1,2^40-1 roundtrip; 2^40 and \u003e5-byte PIV reject; five-byte replay ordering and duplicates work; request/response terminal exhaustion never wraps; debug/release/no_std/all-feature tests, Clippy, and three reviews pass.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-17T22:58:23.329282Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-17T22:58:23.329282Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.48.1","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.48","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-17T22:58:23.478629Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}

new_value: Implemented and validated with RFC-literal tests; debug/release/all-feature/no-default suites and library Clippy pass; three hash-bound reviews found no remaining in-scope P0-P2 defects. Context lifecycle remains .48.2.
